methods.description: The process of distributing quails into experimental groups to study the effects of different densities and dietary supplements.

steps.description: The quails were randomly distributed to 6 experimental groups, three of them (21 birds) were divided into three replicates, 7 birds for each replicate (3×7), and three other groups (75 birds) were also divided into three replicates, 25 birds for each replicate (3×25).
steps.searchable_summary: randomly distribute quails into experimental groups with specified replicates

steps.description: The 1st group of quails was put at a rate of 21 birds/m2 (control), normal density (ND), and fed the basal diet.
steps.searchable_summary: assign control group with normal density and basal diet

steps.description: The 2nd group of quails was put at a rate of 21 birds/m2 and the diet addition with bee pollen (BP) 20 g/kg diet.
steps.searchable_summary: assign group with normal density and bee pollen diet 20 g/kg

steps.description: The 3rd group of quails was put at a rate of 21 birds/m2 and the diet addition with BP 30 g/kg diet.
steps.searchable_summary: assign group with normal density and bee pollen diet 30 g/kg

steps.description: The 4th group high density (HD) of 75 birds/m2 fed the basal diet.
steps.searchable_summary: assign group with high density and basal diet

steps.description: The 5th group high density (HD) of 75 birds/m2 and the diet supplemented with (BP) 20 g/kg diet.
steps.searchable_summary: assign group with high density and bee pollen diet 20 g/kg

steps.description: The 6th group high density (HD) of 75 birds/m2 and the diet supplemented with (BP) 30 g/kg diet.
steps.searchable_summary: assign group with high density and bee pollen diet 30 g/kg
